questions.stem: What does the woman want to do?
questions.context:
W: I'd like to return this printer. It stopped working after just two days.
M: I'm sorry about that. Do you have your receipt?
W: Yes, here it is. I'd prefer a replacement rather than a refund, if possible.
M: Let me check if we have the same model in stock.
questions.explanation: The woman wants to exchange the defective printer for a working one. She says 'I'd prefer a replacement rather than a refund.'

options.text: Get her money back for the printer.
Have the printer repaired.
Exchange the printer for a new one.
Purchase a different model.

questions.stem: According to the man, what will happen next week?
questions.context:
W: Have you heard about the changes to the parking policy?
M: Yes, starting next week, all employees need to display a new parking permit.
W: Where can we pick up the new permits?
M: The security office on the ground floor is distributing them.
questions.explanation: The man says that starting next week, employees need new parking permits. The change is a new permit requirement.

options.text: The parking lot will be closed for repairs.
New parking permits will be required.
Employee parking fees will increase.
Additional parking spaces will be added.

questions.stem: What is the woman's problem?
questions.context:
M: How's the report coming along?
W: I'm having trouble accessing the sales database. My login seems to have expired.
M: That happened to several people after the system update. Contact IT support—they can reset it right away.
W: Thanks, I'll call them now.
questions.explanation: The woman cannot access the sales database because her login expired after a system update.

options.text: She cannot log in to the database.
She lost her report file.
She missed a deadline.

W: The client wants the proposal by Friday, but I haven't finished the financial projections yet.
M: Why don't you ask Kevin to help with the numbers? He's finished his current project.
W: That's a good idea. I'll talk to him after lunch.
M: Also, I can review the final draft if you send it Thursday evening.
questions.explanation: The man suggests asking Kevin for help with the financial projections since Kevin has finished his current project.

options.text: Request a deadline extension from the client.
Ask a colleague for assistance.
Use last year's financial data.
Schedule a meeting with the client.

questions.stem: Why is the man calling?
questions.context:
M: Hello, I'm calling about the job opening for a marketing coordinator that was posted on your website.
W: Yes, that position is still available. Have you submitted an application?
M: I have, but I wanted to ask about the start date. It wasn't listed in the posting.
W: We're hoping the new hire can start by the first of next month.
questions.explanation: The man is calling to ask about the start date for a job he has applied to, as it was not mentioned in the posting.

options.text: To apply for a position.
To check on his application status.
To ask about the start date for a job.
To negotiate the salary.

M: The shipment from our supplier was supposed to arrive this morning.
W: I'll check the tracking system. There could be a delay due to the weather.
M: If it doesn't arrive by three o'clock, we'll need to call the supplier directly.
W: Understood. Let me pull up the tracking information right now.
questions.explanation: The woman says she will check the tracking system immediately to find out about the delayed shipment.

options.text: Call the supplier.
Reschedule the delivery.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply when she says, 'I wish I'd known sooner'?
questions.context:
M: The team dinner has been moved from Thursday to Friday because the restaurant is fully booked.
W: Oh, I wish I'd known sooner. I already have plans for Friday evening.
M: I'm sorry. We could save you a seat in case you can rearrange your schedule.
W: Thanks, I'll see what I can do.
questions.explanation: The woman implies that if she had known earlier about the change, she could have adjusted her plans. She already has a conflict on Friday.

options.text: She would have made a reservation herself.
She might have kept Friday evening free.
She would have chosen a different restaurant.
She would have cancelled the dinner.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of the announcement?

To announce that the store is closing soon.

To welcome customers to a grand opening.

questions.explanation: The speaker describes a project management workshop covering time management and resource allocation, asking for registration by tomorrow.

options.text: It has been postponed until next month.
It will focus on project management skills.
It requires a registration fee.
It is mandatory for all employees.

Please be advised that the water supply will be shut off on Saturday morning from eight to twelve for routine maintenance of the main pipes. We recommend storing water in advance for drinking and cooking. We apologize for any inconvenience and appreciate your cooperation.

options.text: Office workers in a business district.
Residents of an apartment complex.

questions.stem: What should the listeners do before the event?
questions.context: Attention all volunteers for Saturday's community cleanup event. Please meet at the Central Park entrance by eight A.M. Gloves and trash bags will be provided, but we ask that you bring your own water bottle and wear comfortable shoes. If you haven't signed the waiver form yet, please do so when you check in at the registration table.

options.text: Purchase cleaning supplies.
Bring their own water bottles and wear comfortable shoes.
Submit a volunteer application.
Attend an orientation session.

questions.stem: What is being advertised?
questions.context: Are you looking for a faster internet connection? Metro Broadband is now offering our premium fiber optic plan at fifty percent off for the first six months. With speeds up to one gigabit per second, you'll enjoy seamless streaming, gaming, and video calls. Call us today at 555-0199 or visit our website to sign up. This limited-time offer ends March thirty-first.
questions.explanation: The advertisement is promoting a discounted internet service plan from Metro Broadband with fiber optic speeds.

options.text: A mobile phone plan.
A cable television package.
A high-speed internet service.
A computer repair service.

questions.stem: What problem does the speaker mention?
questions.context: Good afternoon, passengers on Flight 472 to Seoul. We regret to inform you that our departure has been delayed by approximately ninety minutes due to a mechanical issue that needs to be inspected. We will provide updated boarding information as soon as it becomes available. In the meantime, meal vouchers will be distributed at the gate counter. We apologize for the inconvenience.
questions.explanation: The speaker reports a flight delay caused by a mechanical issue that needs inspection.

options.text: Bad weather is affecting the flight.
The flight is overbooked.
A mechanical issue has delayed the departure.
The crew has not arrived yet.

questions.stem: What will happen at the end of this month?
questions.context: I'm pleased to announce that our new company cafeteria will open on the last day of this month. The cafeteria will serve breakfast from seven to nine and lunch from eleven thirty to one thirty. All employees will receive a twenty-percent discount during the opening week. Meal plans can be purchased at the HR office starting next Monday.
questions.explanation: The speaker says the new company cafeteria will open on the last day of this month.

options.text: The office building will be renovated.
A new company cafeteria will open.
The lunch break hours will change.
New employees will start orientation.

questions.stem: All employees must submit their expense reports _______ the end of each month.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition with 'the end of each month' is 'by', meaning no later than.

options.text: by
until
since

questions.stem: The marketing team has been working _______ on the product launch campaign.
questions.explanation: 'Diligently' is an adverb that modifies the verb 'working,' describing how the team has been working.

options.text: diligent
diligently
diligence
diligences

questions.stem: The shipment _______ expected to arrive at the warehouse by next Tuesday.
questions.explanation: 'The shipment' is a singular subject, so 'is' is the correct form of the verb 'to be.'

options.text: is
are
were
have been

questions.stem: Ms. Park, _______ has extensive experience in accounting, will lead the audit team.
questions.explanation: 'Who' is the correct relative pronoun for a person serving as the subject of the relative clause.

options.text: who
which
whom
whose

questions.stem: The new regulations will be _______ effective starting from the first of January.
questions.explanation: 'Made' collocates with 'effective' to mean officially enacted or implemented.

options.text: turned
brought
made
kept

questions.stem: The budget proposal was approved _______ it included detailed cost projections.
questions.explanation: 'Because' introduces a reason clause explaining why the proposal was approved.

options.text: although
because
unless
while

questions.stem: Applicants should demonstrate their ability to work _______ in a team setting.
questions.explanation: 'Effectively' is an adverb that describes how one works. 'Effective' would be an adjective.

options.text: effective
effectiveness
effectively
effecting

questions.stem: _______ we receive your payment, your subscription will be activated within twenty-four hours.
questions.explanation: 'Once' means 'as soon as' and introduces a condition/time clause.

options.text: Once
Despite
During
Rather

questions.stem: The company provides health insurance for all of _______ full-time employees.
questions.explanation: 'Its' is the possessive pronoun for 'the company,' which is a singular entity.

options.text: its
it's
their
it

questions.stem: The factory output increased _______ despite the recent supply chain disruptions.
questions.explanation: 'Significantly' is an adverb modifying the verb 'increased.'

options.text: significant
significance
significantly
signified

questions.stem: The director asked each department head to prepare a _______ summary of quarterly results.
questions.explanation: 'Brief' is an adjective that modifies the noun 'summary.'

options.text: brief
briefly
briefed
briefness

questions.stem: All visitors are required to wear identification badges _______ they are on company premises.
questions.explanation: 'While' introduces a time clause meaning 'during the time that.'

options.text: while
although
before
unless

questions.stem: Dear valued customer, thank you for your recent purchase of our premium office chair. We hope you are _______ with the quality and comfort of your new chair.
questions.context:
Dear valued customer,

Thank you for your recent purchase of our premium office chair. We hope you are _______ with the quality and comfort of your new chair. If you have any concerns, our customer service team is available Monday through Friday from 9 A.M. to 6 P.M.

As a token of our appreciation, we are offering you a 15% discount on your next purchase. Simply enter the code THANKYOU15 at checkout.

Best regards,
Bright Office Furniture
questions.explanation: 'Satisfied' fits the context of hoping the customer is happy with a purchased product.

options.text: satisfy
satisfied
satisfying
satisfaction

questions.stem: The annual company picnic will take place on Saturday, June 14, at Riverside Park. All employees and their families are _______ to attend.
questions.context:
Subject: Annual Company Picnic

The annual company picnic will take place on Saturday, June 14, at Riverside Park. All employees and their families are _______ to attend. Food, drinks, and entertainment will be provided free of charge.

Please RSVP by June 7 so that we can make appropriate arrangements. Activities will include a softball game, a three-legged race, and a raffle with exciting prizes.

We look forward to seeing everyone there!
questions.explanation: 'Welcome' fits the sentence meaning employees are invited and encouraged to attend.

options.text: welcome
requiring
prevented
hesitant

questions.stem: We are pleased to announce that our new flagship store in downtown Portland will _______ on March 1.
questions.context:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

We are pleased to announce that our new flagship store in downtown Portland will _______ on March 1. The 5,000-square-foot location on Elm Street features our complete product line, including exclusive items not available online.

To celebrate the grand opening, we will offer 20% off all purchases during the first week. Customers who visit on opening day will also receive a complimentary tote bag.

Contact: Lisa Park, Public Relations, 555-0234
questions.explanation: 'Open' is the correct verb form after 'will' in the future tense, meaning the store will begin operating.

options.text: open
opening
opened
opens

questions.stem: All employees should submit their vacation requests at least two weeks _______ their planned leave dates.
questions.context:
INTERNAL MEMO
To: All Staff
Subject: Updated Vacation Policy

Effective immediately, all employees should submit their vacation requests at least two weeks _______ their planned leave dates. Requests should be submitted through the HR portal and must be approved by your direct supervisor.

Please note that vacation days cannot be carried over to the following year. Unused days will be forfeited on December 31.

Human Resources Department
questions.explanation: 'Before' correctly indicates the time relationship—requests must be submitted prior to leave dates.

options.text: before
since
during
after

questions.stem: Greenfield Technologies has _______ announced plans to expand its research facility in Austin, Texas.
questions.context:
BUSINESS NEWS

Greenfield Technologies has _______ announced plans to expand its research facility in Austin, Texas. The $40 million expansion will add 200 new positions over the next three years.

The company cited growing demand for its renewable energy products as the primary reason for the investment. Construction is expected to begin in the second quarter of this year.

'This expansion reflects our commitment to innovation and job creation,' said CEO Martin Hughes.
questions.explanation: 'Recently' is an adverb that appropriately modifies 'announced,' indicating the announcement was made not long ago.

options.text: recent
recently
recency
recenter

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the notice?
questions.context:
NOTICE TO ALL TENANTS

The building management would like to inform all tenants that the elevators in Tower A will undergo maintenance on Saturday, April 12, from 8:00 A.M. to 5:00 P.M. During this time, only the stairs will be available for access to upper floors.

We understand this may cause inconvenience, especially for residents on higher floors. If you require special assistance, please contact the front desk at extension 200 before Friday, April 11.

We appreciate your patience and cooperation.

Building Management Office
questions.explanation: The notice informs tenants about scheduled elevator maintenance and the temporary unavailability of elevators.

options.text: To announce a rent increase.
To inform tenants about elevator maintenance.
To introduce new building security rules.
To invite tenants to a community meeting.

questions.stem: What is indicated about the position?
questions.context:
JOB OPENING: Marketing Associate
Horizon Media Group

We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Marketing Associate to join our growing team. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of experience in digital marketing, including social media management and content creation.

Responsibilities include developing marketing campaigns, analyzing performance metrics, and collaborating with the design team on promotional materials.

This is a full-time position with competitive salary and benefits. Remote work is available two days per week.

To apply, send your resume and portfolio to careers@horizonmedia.com by April 30.
questions.explanation: The job posting mentions 'Remote work is available two days per week,' indicating it allows some remote work.

options.text: It is a part-time opportunity.
It requires five years of experience.
It allows remote work on some days.
It is located overseas.

questions.stem: According to the email, what does Mr. Tanaka request?
questions.context:
From: Kenji Tanaka <k.tanaka@globalcorp.com>
To: Sarah Mitchell <s.mitchell@globalcorp.com>
Date: March 18
Subject: Q1 Sales Report

Dear Sarah,

Thank you for sending the preliminary Q1 sales figures. The data looks promising, but I noticed that the figures for the Asia-Pacific region seem lower than expected.

Could you double-check those numbers with the regional office before we present the report to the board next Thursday? I'd also appreciate it if you could add a comparison chart showing year-over-year trends for each region.

Please have the updated version ready by Tuesday afternoon so I can review it before the meeting.

Best regards,
Kenji Tanaka
Vice President, Sales
questions.explanation: Mr. Tanaka asks Sarah to verify the Asia-Pacific numbers with the regional office and add a year-over-year comparison chart.

options.text: That the board meeting be postponed.
That certain sales figures be verified.
That all regional offices be contacted.
That a new sales strategy be developed.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Greenleaf Catering?
questions.context:
CUSTOMER REVIEW — Greenleaf Catering
Reviewed by: Maria Gonzalez
Rating: ★★★★☆

We hired Greenleaf Catering for our company's year-end celebration with approximately 80 guests. The menu featured a variety of vegetarian and non-vegetarian options, all beautifully presented.

The main courses were excellent, particularly the grilled salmon and the mushroom risotto. However, dessert was limited to just two choices, which was a bit disappointing for such a large event.

The staff was professional and responsive throughout the evening. Despite the minor dessert issue, I would recommend Greenleaf for corporate events and would consider using them again.

Price: $$$ (premium)
questions.explanation: The reviewer gave 4 out of 5 stars and said she would recommend them and use them again despite the minor dessert issue, indicating they provide generally high-quality service.

options.text: It specializes in dessert catering.
It only serves vegetarian food.
It generally provides quality service.
It is the most affordable option available.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of the message?
questions.context:
TEXT MESSAGE CHAIN

James [2:15 PM]: Hi Priya, just confirming our site visit tomorrow at the Elm Street property. Can you meet me there at 10 AM?

Priya [2:22 PM]: Sure, 10 AM works. Should I bring the inspection checklist from last time?

James [2:24 PM]: Yes, please. Also bring the updated floor plans. The client wants to discuss possible modifications to the layout.

Priya [2:30 PM]: Got it. I'll print everything out tonight. See you there!
questions.explanation: James is confirming the details of a meeting/site visit at a property with Priya.

options.text: To cancel an appointment.
To confirm details of a site visit.
To request time off from work.
To order building materials.

questions.stem: In the article, the word 'sustainable' in paragraph 2, line 1, is closest in meaning to:
questions.context:
LOCAL BUSINESS NEWS

The city council approved a proposal from Westfield Development to build a mixed-use complex on the former industrial site at 400 Harbor Drive. The $120 million project will include 250 residential units, ground-floor retail space, and a community center.

The development will use sustainable building practices, including solar panels, rainwater collection systems, and energy-efficient insulation. Construction is set to begin in September and is expected to take approximately two years to complete.

Mayor Helen Torres praised the project, saying it will create jobs and revitalize the waterfront area. Some residents have expressed concerns about increased traffic, and the council has agreed to conduct a traffic impact study before issuing final permits.
questions.explanation: 'Sustainable' in this context means 'environmentally responsible' or 'able to be maintained without harming the environment.'

options.text: Affordable.
Environmentally responsible.
Quickly completed.
Government-funded.

questions.stem: What is suggested about Apex Consulting?
questions.context:
From: David Lee <d.lee@apexconsulting.com>
To: Yuki Sato <y.sato@nipponsteel.co.jp>
Date: April 2
Subject: Follow-up on March 28 Meeting

Dear Ms. Sato,

Thank you for taking the time to meet with our team last week. We were impressed by your company's expansion plans for the Southeast Asian market.

As discussed, Apex Consulting can provide market research, regulatory analysis, and local partnership facilitation. I've attached a detailed proposal outlining our services, timeline, and fees.

We have successfully guided fifteen companies through similar market entry strategies in the region over the past five years. Our consulting fee has tiered pricing based on project scope.

Please review the proposal and let us know if you'd like to schedule a follow-up call. We look forward to the possibility of working together.

Best regards,
David Lee
Senior Consultant, Apex Consulting
questions.explanation: The email mentions Apex has guided 15 companies through market entry strategies in the past 5 years, suggesting they have extensive experience in international market entry.

options.text: It is a newly established firm.
It has experience helping companies enter new markets.
It is based in Southeast Asia.
It only works with Japanese companies.
